name: Timed Challenge
description: Timed Challenge is a temporal mechanic that changes speed, ordering, deadlines, cooldowns, or history.
design_notes: Timed Challenge should be designed around a clear player promise, a readable trigger, and a predictable result. For time mechanics, the important design work is deciding when the mechanic is allowed, how much commitment it creates, what feedback confirms success, and what counter-pressure keeps it from solving every situation. Strong tuning makes the mechanic feel intentional even when it is used repeatedly or combined with adjacent systems.
design_purpose: Give the player a readable way to use timed challenge as part of the core loop.
Create meaningful choices around timing, cost, risk, positioning, or long-term planning.
Connect timed challenge to adjacent systems so it supports the larger game design instead of acting as an isolated feature.
player_fantasy: The player feels they can manipulate urgency, sequence, or consequence.

parameters.name: time_scale
parameters.description: Multiplier applied to game time or affected systems.

parameters.name: duration
parameters.description: How long the temporal effect remains active.

parameters.name: recovery_or_cooldown
parameters.description: Delay before the mechanic can be used again.

edge_cases: Timed Challenge is triggered during a scene transition, pause, respawn, or save/load boundary.
Timed Challenge overlaps with another high-priority state such as damage, stun, dialogue, menu focus, or cutscene control.
Timed Challenge runs at extreme frame rates, high latency, very large coordinates, or after long play sessions.
common_bugs: Timed Challenge state does not reset correctly after interruption or reload.
Feedback for Timed Challenge plays even when the mechanic fails or is rejected.
Timed Challenge produces different results between frame rates, platforms, or network clients.
balancing_notes: Make Timed Challenge strong enough to justify attention but limited enough that other mechanics keep their purpose.
Tune costs, cooldowns, ranges, and feedback together; changing only one number can shift the mechanic from optional to dominant.
accessibility_notes: Provide clear visual, audio, or haptic feedback when Timed Challenge becomes available, succeeds, or fails.
Avoid relying on a single sensory cue or extremely narrow timing unless the game offers assists or alternate settings.

implementation_notes.unity: Implement Timed Challenge with engine-agnostic state first, then bind it to MonoBehaviour, CharacterController, Rigidbody, UI Toolkit, or Netcode systems as appropriate.
implementation_notes.godot: Model Timed Challenge as a node or resource-driven state and update deterministic pieces from _physics_process when timing or collision matters.
implementation_notes.unreal: Implement Timed Challenge with Actor Components, CharacterMovement, Gameplay Ability System, UMG, or replicated gameplay state depending on the mechanic scope.
implementation_notes.web: Implement Timed Challenge as data-owned state updated from the main loop; keep rendering, input, and simulation boundaries explicit for Canvas, Phaser, Three.js, or custom JavaScript.
